title: cytisus eriocarpus subsp. tmoleus
intro: Cytisus eriocarpus subsp. tmoleus, also known as Spanish broom, is a shrub-like legume native to the Iberian Peninsula. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Cytisus eriocarpus subsp. tmoleus include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit immunomodulatory effects. Cytisus eriocarpus subsp. tmoleus is often prepared as an herbal infusion, tincture, or decoction, and has been traditionally used to treat various conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
